t_ch: 还有传言说，有人只是稍微翻开《瘈狂之卷》看了一眼，就丢掉了性命！
t_en: I've even heard rumors about someone losing their life just because they stole a peek into the Manuscript of Delirium!

t_ch: 你放心，我会小心的。
t_en: Don't worry. I will be careful.

t_ch: 没错，千万要小心。我俩好歹是搞科学的，起初不太相信这些东西……
t_en: Yeah, that's the attitude. Being students of the sciences, we thought those were just baseless rumors...

t_ch: 这故事蛮假的。
t_en: Your story is hardly convincing.

t_ch: 是啊，我们一开始也是这么想的，觉得肯定是哪个古人在乱编故事吓唬人。
t_en: Well, that's what we thought at first, too. We believed it was nothing more than some wild stories.

t_ch: 我男朋友阿云，也就是躺在地上那位，他壮着胆子翻开了书……
t_en: So my boyfriend, Yun... Yeah, the guy lying on the ground now... summoned up the courage and opened the book...

t_ch: …可下一秒立刻就瞪直了双眼，全身抽搐、蜷缩成一团，然后口齿不清地喊了一句「别靠近我」之类的话…然后就…晕过去了……
t_en: ...But the next second, his eyes opened wide and his body twitched and curled into a ball. Then he slurred something like "Don't come closer!"... and then he passed out...

t_ch: 这真的太可怕了…我们再也不敢打禁忌书典的主意了…您要回收这本书，也请一定要小心行事啊！我先带着他去医馆了！
t_en: This is terrible... We'll never dare to tamper with any of the forbidden tomes again. Please be careful while retrieving it! I'll take my boyfriend to the apothecary!

t_ch: 下次别偷东西了。
t_en: Don't steal anything ever again.

t_ch: 我们绝对再也不敢了！
t_en: No! We'll never do it again!

t_ch: 路上小心。
t_en: Safe travels.

t_ch: 您才是一定要小心啊！
t_en: Please, do be careful!

t_ch: 这大概就是玉络让我回收的《瘈狂之卷》了。刚才那两个偷书的学生不像在演戏，难道这书真有那么邪门？
t_en: This must be the Manuscript of Delirium that Yuluo asked me to retrieve. I don't think those two students were pretending, but does this book really possess such dangerous power?

t_ch: （放回原地）
t_en: (Put it back where you found it.)

t_ch: （安全起见，放进背包）
t_en: (Put it in your inventory just to be safe.)

t_ch: 既然读这本书有这么大的危险性，那我还是稳妥些，不要翻开它了吧。直接拿去带给玉络就好。
t_en: If reading this book could be so dangerous, I'll play it safe and bring it straight back to Yuluo.

t_ch: （翻开看看）
t_en: (Open it and take a look.)

t_ch: 翻开书的一瞬间，你感受到一阵强烈的刺痛。
t_en: The moment you open the book, you feel an acute, penetrating pain throughout your body.

t_ch: 你眼前一黑，全身肌肉无法控制地紧绷在一起，几乎跌坐在地。
t_en: As everything goes black, your muscles tense up uncontrollably and you nearly stumble over.

t_ch: 这是一段不属于你的记忆，就像是你读取忆泡时一样，你感受到的疯狂是外来的，而非本源性的…逐渐的，你对周围世界的感受也开始重合……
t_en: It is as if you are experiencing someone else's memory, much like reading a Memory Bubble. A hint of delirium seeps in from outside, rather than from within yourself. Gradually, your perception of the world around you begins to blur...

t_ch: 你看清周围仿佛是雪夜，这里不是仙舟罗浮，一辆失控的汽车跌跌撞撞，在湿滑的地面上难以自我控制，它不出意料地撞向了你，你飞向了半空中……
t_en: As you take in your surroundings, you find yourself in a snowy night and realize that you are no longer on the Xianzhou Luofu. Suddenly, a car loses control on the slippery road, careening towards you with terrifying speed and sending you hurling through the air...

t_ch: 你忍着痛，从车窗玻璃的反射上看清了自己的相貌——那毫无疑问是一个身穿制服的死鱼眼少年，此刻你*毫不怀疑*那就是自己，那本不应该是你自己。
t_en: Despite the excruciating pain that you endure, you manage to catch a glimpse of yourself in the car window's reflection. You see the face of a lifeless teenager in school uniform staring back at you. As surreal as it may seem, you know with "certainty" that it is your reflection...

t_ch: 渐渐地，你的文字开始崩坏…涓轰簡缁欐瘝浜叉姤浠囨潃姝讳粬……
t_en: The Words iN FrONt oF yoUR eYes StaRT tO DiSTorT, dISsolVe, anNnnNd tHene TuUrRN iNto ssOmmmooMmmeTHing ILLeeegigbla...

t_ch: 仿佛幸运女神眷顾，从你手里掉落的书本巧合地合上了，书合上的一瞬间，不适感也烟消云散了。你用了数秒才勉强稳住心神。
t_en: Fortunately, by pure chance, the book falls from your hand and closes. At that moment, the discomfort immediately dissipates. It takes you a few moments to regain your composure.

t_ch: 那场逼真的车祸足以让你相信，《瘈狂之卷》能让人将记忆强行植入他人记忆之中，恐怕这本书里留下的都是危险的体验。
t_en: The immersive car accident makes you believe that the Manuscript of Delirium possesses the power to transplant foreign memories into others. Perhaps the book is full of experiences about being on the brink of death.

t_ch: 你交还了《瘈狂之卷》并讲述了回收它的过程。
t_en: You return the Manuscript of Delirium and explain how you've retrieved it.

t_ch: 这书原来是被那几个实习的学生给偷走了…我还在想呢，要是丹枢大人的那些残党，肯定都知道这书没什么价值，不会去偷的。
t_en: So it was stolen by those interns... You know, I was wondering why Lady Dan Shu's remnants would steal this book, because they should be well aware that this book is totally worthless.

t_ch: 这书其实就是记载了很多不应对外公布的丹方。前人们为了防止这些东西泄露，就在书上设置了一些小机关，你碰到的应该是电流吧？
t_en: This book actually contains a lot of prescriptions that shouldn't be made public. The author added some little gadgets to prevent its secrets from being leaked. I believe you were knocked out by an electric current, right?

t_ch: 「一旦阅读者未经过校验，这本书就会放出强大的电流，将读者放倒……」
t_en: "Once an unauthorized reader opens this book, it will release a powerful electric shock and knock them out..."

t_ch: 至少我听说的版本是这样的，强行植入他人的记忆，这种事情天方夜谭，除非有忆庭的人才能做到…等等，好像也有可能？
t_en: At least, this is what I've heard. As for "transplanting memories," that's simply too wild to be true. It's impossible without the help of the Garden of Recollection... Wait, what if they're really involved?

t_ch: 算了，这种恐怖传说至少能避免一些脑子不清楚的年轻人，对「禁忌书典」产生什么不该有的好奇心。
t_en: Well, anyway, these horror legends will keep young people with unwanted curiosity about the forbidden tomes at bay.

t_ch: 这样一来，丹鼎司丢失的所有「禁忌书典」就都已经找齐了。多亏了你帮忙，这些书才没产生任何不良影响。
t_en: Thanks to your help, all the lost forbidden tomes have been safely returned without causing any calamities.

t_ch: 青镞大人果然慧眼识英才。若不是有你在，这三本书我真不知道要找到什么时候去……
t_en: Madam Qingzu was right to have faith in you. Without your help, it would have taken me forever to find these lost manuscripts...

t_ch: 这是你应得的报酬，收下吧。以后可能还会有事情拜托你，到时候还请多多帮助了。
t_en: Here's your hard-earned payment. I might need your help again in the future. See you then.

t_ch: 我这也太倒霉了…
t_en: I'm jinxed...

t_ch: 这世界太不公平…
t_en: This world has been so unfair...

t_ch: 我这也太倒霉了…世界对我实在是太不公平了，这日子没法过了……
t_en: I'm jinxed... This world has been so unfair... I just can't go on like this...

t_ch: 不要丧失生活的希望啊！
t_en: Don't give up on your life!

t_ch: 我也想啊！可这残酷的命运不给我这个机会啊！
t_en: I didn't want to, but now I'm feeling like giving in to the cruel hand of fate!

t_ch: 出什么事了吗？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 哎，别提了…我实在是太倒霉了，倒霉到快要过不下去了……
t_en: Well... I'm just so unlucky... I can't take it anymore...

t_ch: 我也觉得日子没法过了…
t_en: I understand. I can't take it anymore, either...

t_ch: 别别别，你该过还是得过…我过不下去，是因为我真的太倒霉了……
t_en: No, no, no, you've got to keep going... but I really can't take it anymore, because I'm just so unlucky...

t_ch: 我叫做阿幸，人如其名，非常不幸。你主动来关心我，说明你是个好人，还是离我远些，免得我的坏运气连累了你。
t_en: My name is A-Xing, which means "fortunate." However, I'm positively jinxed. I appreciate that you're concerned for me, but you seem like a good person and I don't want to drag you down.

t_ch: 没关系，我不信这套的。
t_en: Don't worry. Bad luck or jinxes don't really exist.

t_ch: 我原本也不信这套的啊！直到现实一次次给我响亮的耳光……
t_en: I thought so, too, but then reality kept slapping me in the face and proving me wrong, time after time...

t_ch: 你在鉴爻轩门口干什么？
t_en: What are you doing here, at the entrance of the Looking Bronze?

t_ch: 我也不知道…可能是想在这里给自己盖棺定论吧……
t_en: I don't know... Maybe I just want to get a final conclusion this time...

t_ch: 我也算是这鉴爻轩的常客了，每隔一段时间都要来抽一次签，可你猜怎么着？我抽到的永远都是那三种凶签！几百次了，一次不凶的都没抽到过！
t_en: I've been coming to the Looking Bronze every once in a while, but guess what? I got one of the three "Woe" sticks every single time! I've done this hundreds of times, and not once have I got a "Weal!"

t_ch: 那就别抽了…
t_en: Why don't you just stop...?

t_ch: 不，还是要抽的…凶签和凶签之间也有区别。
t_en: No, I've got to do this... Even though they're all "Woe" sticks, there are differences between them.

t_ch: 鉴爻轩不准的…
t_en: The fortune-telling here is never accurate...

t_ch: 不，若月老板太准了。
t_en: That's not true. Ruoyue is an excellent fortune-teller.

t_ch: 哇哦。
t_en: Wow.

t_ch: 你不会是在幸灾乐祸吧？我是认真的。
t_en: You're not gloating, are you? I'm serious.

t_ch: 就比如说前几天吧，我抽中了「苍城」签，这个卦象的意思是「突如其来的惨烈打击」……
t_en: For example, I drew the "Cangcheng" hexagram the other day, which indicates "a sudden and tragic blow"...

t_ch: 然后我刚离开鉴爻轩，就被机巧鸟撞到头，伤势严重。之后去医馆换药，结果那个医馆是药王秘传的窝点，正赶上云骑军收网，又阴差阳错把我当成了同党，抓走审了一天。
t_en: Then right after I left the Looking Bronze, I got hit on the head by a cycrane and suffered a nasty wound, so I went to an apothecary to get it treated, but guess what? That apothecary turned out to be a hideout for the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus, and the Cloud Knights mistook me for an accomplice and took me away for a whole day of trial.

t_ch: 总算是解释清楚了身份吧…可这天还刚好是我学宫的毕业考试！等到云骑把我放了的时候，考试都结束了半天了！
t_en: To make matters worse, my graduation exam at the academy happened to be on that very same day... By the time they finally let me go, the exam was already over.

t_ch: 我想你也听明白了，我就是这样一个不幸的人。我今天要抽人生最后一次签，如果还是凶签，我的人生就已经盖棺定论了！
t_en: I think you get what I'm trying to say: I'm simply jinxed. Today, I'm going to draw one last time, and if it's still a "Woe" hexagram, I might as well give up on my life!

t_ch: 这次无论如何都要抽到吉签！
t_en: I must get a "Weal" hexagram this time no matter what!

t_ch: 阿幸嘴里念念叨叨，看来他正在酝酿情绪。他还没有准备好去迎接自己的命运。
t_en: A-Xing is muttering and trying to gather his courage. Apparently, he is not yet ready to unveil his fate.

t_ch: 要不你去确认一下，世界上真的有这么倒霉的人吗？
t_en: Maybe you should ask Ruoyue if there are actually people who are born unlucky?

t_ch: （趁阿幸去抽签之前，找若月老板问问吧…）
t_en: (I should check with Ruoyue before A-Xing draws...)

t_ch: 吉吉吉吉…
t_en: Weal, weal, weal...

t_ch: 宝贝、宝贝、宝贝…
t_en: Good luck, good luck, good luck...

t_ch: 吉吉吉吉…
t_en: Weal, weal, weal...

t_ch: 你别这样看着我，我确实说过，我要去抽人生最后一次签。
t_en: Don't give me that look. I did say I was going to draw one last time in my life.

t_ch: …可是我现在还不敢，除非帝弓能保佑我抽出吉签，我才能下定决心去抽签。
t_en: ...But I don't dare to do it yet, unless the Reignbow Arbiter can guarantee that I'll get a "Weal."

t_ch: 你认识倒霉的阿幸吗？
t_en: Have you heard of the jinxed A-Xing?

t_ch: 您要只说「阿幸」，我还真未必能想起来。但您要加上「倒霉的」这个定语，那就没别人了…他每次的卜卦都是凶签，但还是坚持不懈地卜卦。
t_en: A-Xing? Well, I may not remember the name along, but since you mentioned "jinxed," I can hardly forget someone as unlucky as him... I mean, he has never drawn a "Weal" stick, but he just keeps on trying anyway.

t_ch: 他不是因为抽出了凶签才倒霉，而是因为倒霉，才会次次都抽出凶签。当然我推荐了他很多转运道具，不过他的霉运有些…过强了。
t_en: It's his bad luck that makes him draw a "Woe" stick every time, not the other way around. Of course, I've recommended him a lot of charms and trinkets to help improve his luck, but his bad luck is a bit too... overwhelming.

t_ch: 唉…其实我呢，一直觉得人的命运绝非天注定。人如果能用更好的心态来面对未来，自然就会面对更好的未来。
t_en: To be honest... I've always believed that one's fate is not necessarily predestined. If one can stay optimistic about their future, then they will have a brighter future ahead of them.

t_ch: 如果阿幸能抽出一次不那么凶的签，也许就能改变他面对命运的心态…可惜，他一直没等来这个机会……
t_en: If A-Xing could experience something other than "Woe," it might help change his attitude about his fate... Unfortunately, he's never had the chance...

t_ch: 我们钦定一个吉签给他呗？
t_en: How about we guarantee him a "Weal"?

t_ch: 嗯？您的意思是…我来人为修改占卜的结果，确保他一定会抽到一个吉签？可这只是给了他一个伪造的抽签结果啊，又没办法真的让他变幸运……
t_en: Huh? Are you suggesting... that I rig the results so that A-Xing gets a "Weal"? But that's just a fake result. It's not really going to change his bad luck...

t_ch: 你说命运绝非天注定。
t_en: You just said that one's fate is not necessarily predestined.

t_ch: 唔…确实是这个道理。
t_en: Well... I guess that's a good point.

t_ch: 只要能改变他的心态就好。
t_en: As long as this helps change his attitude about his fate.

t_ch: 唔…确实是这个道理。
t_en: Well... I guess that's a good point.

t_ch: 他说这是人生最后一签…
t_en: He said this would be the last draw of his life...

t_ch: 我有些动摇了。
t_en: I'm sort of convinced, I guess.

t_ch: 唉，不管那么多了！万一给了他吉签，他还是倒霉，那可不是咱俩的错啊！
t_en: All right, fine! Just don't blame me or yourself if we guarantee him a "Weal" stick but he still has bad luck!

t_ch: 稍等我片刻啊，我改造一下占卜用的玉兆。
t_en: Wait for a while. I'll need to modify the jade abacus for divination.

t_ch: 若月开始改造用于占卜的玉兆。她毕竟曾是太卜司的卜者，调整这些硬件设备也是得心应手。
t_en: Ruoyue starts to modify her jade abacus used for divination. It doesn't take her too long to alter the hardware, since she used to be a diviner in the Divination Commission.

t_ch: 行了，改造完了。您先测试一下改造后的效果？理论上，这个玉兆应该只会出现吉签了。
t_en: All right, it's done. Would you like to test it out? Theoretically, this jade abacus should only produce "Weal" results now.

t_ch: （卜算命运）
t_en: (Divine your fate)

t_ch: 眼观鼻鼻观心，你在心中默念着自己所问之事。
t_en: You dive into the depths of your mind and meditate on the question in your mind.

t_ch: 玉兆开始转动，某种看不见的屏障隔绝了你与外界的联系。在突如其来的宁静里，某个模糊的画面渐渐清晰了起来——
t_en: As the jade abacus starts spinning, some kind of invisible barrier separates you from the outside world. In the midst of the sudden tranquility, a blurred yet unmistakable image gradually becomes clear...

t_ch: 你看见的是仙舟「罗浮」，云骑军拱卫着遮天蔽日的建木，化外民登舟求访，星槎海中人潮汹涌声浪滔天。
t_en: You are seeing the Xianzhou Luofu, on which the towering Ambrosial Arbor looms over countless Cloud Knights. With the arrival of numerous Outworlders, Starskiff Haven is alive with people from all walks of life.

t_ch: 成了，是「罗浮」签！这可是大大的吉签啊！来吧，咱们再试一次。
t_en: It's the "Luofu," a very auspicious stick! Awesome, let's try again!

t_ch: （卜算命运）
t_en: (Divine your fate)

t_ch: 不是说「一日不二卦」吗？
t_en: But I thought you should never perform two divinations in a single day?

t_ch: 老板您就别逗了，咱俩都直接修改玉兆了，什么二卦不二卦的还值得纠结吗？
t_en: Come on! Why even bother with these rules when we've already cracked the jade abacus?

t_ch: 玉兆开始转动，某种看不见的屏障隔绝了你与外界的联系。在突如其来的宁静里，某个模糊的画面渐渐清晰了起来——
t_en: As the jade abacus starts spinning, some kind of invisible barrier separates you from the outside world. In the midst of the sudden tranquility, a blurred yet unmistakable image gradually becomes clear...

t_ch: 你看见的是仙舟「曜青」，它挟风驭雷，自九天之外疾驰而来。甲板上无数狐人披甲站立，威风凛凛势不可挡。
t_en: You are seeing the Xianzhou Yaoqing, which is advancing at a dazzling speed, carrying the momentum of tempest and thunder. Countless foxians, fully armed, are standing on the deck in a majestic posture.

t_ch: 没问题没问题，这个也是吉签！唉，虽然好久没摆弄过硬件了，但看起来我的手艺还没完全荒废……
t_en: Awesome, another "Weal" stick! Hah, even though it's been a while since I've tinkered with hardware like this, my skills haven't gone to waste after all...

t_ch: 您找个由头把阿幸叫过来吧。
t_en: Now, please call A-Xing over.

t_ch: 为啥要等我去叫他？
t_en: Why do you need me to call him?

t_ch: 呃…我去叫他显得十分刻意不是吗？「我这占卜用的玉兆好了，你可以来抽签了」…这不是就像是在说「以前玉兆是坏的」一样吗？
t_en: Well... it might seem a bit too deliberate if I just call him out of the blue and say something like, "Hey, my jade abacus is ready. Come over here and draw your lot." It might make it seem like my abacus wasn't ready before.

t_ch: 你去找他吧，尽量假装不知情，咱俩这演技要是不过关，怎么能帮到阿幸嘛。
t_en: Just call him over and pretend you know nothing about my jade abacus. We've got to put on a credible show, otherwise we won't be able to help him.

t_ch: 我要去抽人生最后一次签…可是我好害怕！
t_en: This will be the last fortune in my life... but I'm so scared!

t_ch: 放心抽吧，不会有事的。
t_en: Don't worry. Everything will just be fine.

t_ch: 你不做决定，霉运不会改变。
t_en: Time to make a decision. Your jinx is in your own hands.

t_ch: 你想说的是「命运不会改变」吧，我明白了。
t_en: I think what you were trying to say is, "your fate is in your own hands," right? But yeah, I understand.

t_ch: 其实每一次抽签，我都默默告诉自己「每一次抽签都当最后一次，也不要怕是凶签」…但是，这一次我真的要把它当成最后一次时，我却怂了。
t_en: To be honest, every time I drew a stick, I kept telling myself that it would be the last time and that I shouldn't be discouraged if it turned out to be a "Woe"... But now that I'm actually going to draw the last one, I'm feeling unsure of myself.

t_ch: 我这心理素质，还需要陌生人帮我加油鼓劲…哎，我已经决定了，我要去抽，我要看清命运，我要决定我的未来，我现在就出发！
t_en: I even need a stranger to cheer me up... All right. I've made up my mind! I've got to draw the last lot and embrace my fate, no matter what it may have in store for me!

t_ch: 若月老板，我好怕啊，要是这一抽，这一抽不出，我就、我就……
t_en: I'm still so scared, Ruoyue. What if I still get a "Woe?" I—I will be so...

t_ch: 我算到您这次无论如何都不会抽到凶签了。老板，您要相信我若月的本事啊！
t_en: Don't worry. I've foreseen that you'll definitely not get a "Woe" this time. You should have faith in me!

t_ch: 无论如何都不会抽到凶签了？真的假的？你这卜算这么精准的吗？
t_en: Really? I definitely won't get a "Woe" this time? Do you really mean it?

t_ch: 总之，请老板相信我。我若月一辈子还没算错过什么东西呢！您快占卜一下试试看吧～
t_en: Anyway, please trust me. My divination has never been wrong! Just draw your lot and see what awaits you.

t_ch: 好…好的吧…既然你都这么说了，那我就试试看吧。
t_en: F—Fine... I'll give it a try if you say so.

t_ch: 阿幸站在玉兆前，低下头，大概是在默念自己想要卜算的事情。玉兆开始转动…可转了几圈，玉兆便停转了。
t_en: A-Xing stands in front of the jade abacus and lowers his head, deep in thought about what he wants to divine. The jade abacus starts to turn... but it stops abruptly after only a short while.

t_ch: 嗯？怎么回事？
t_en: Hmmm? What's happening?

t_ch: 阿幸愣了一下，接着继续低头默念。玉兆重新开始转动…然后很快，玉兆又停转了。
t_en: A-Xing freezes for a moment and then lowers his head again. The jade abacus starts to turn... but once again, it stops after a short while.

t_ch: 若月小姐…这…这是怎么一回事啊？
t_en: Wh—What's going on here... Ruoyue...?

t_ch: 出什么问题了吗？
t_en: Is there anything wrong?

t_ch: 老板，你的玉兆不行啊…
t_en: Ruoyue, your jade abacus is not working...

t_ch: 这这这…怎么回事？稍等我看看…啊…这个占卜玉兆…似乎是…坏掉了……
t_en: Wh—Wh—What's going on here? Let me take a look... Ah, this jade abacus... I think it's... broken...

t_ch: 那个…阿幸老板啊，这个玉兆好像坏掉了，不好意思让你白跑了一趟啊……
t_en: I'm sorry, A-Xing... but this abacus seems to be broken... I'm sorry for having you come all this way for nothing...

t_ch: 唉…哈哈哈哈哈哈哈，原来「无论如何都不会抽到凶签」是这么个意思啊？若月小姐，你的卜算确实是灵验的。
t_en: H—Haha... Hahahahahaha! So that's what you meant by "you'll definitely not get a Woe!" You're indeed an excellent fortune-teller, Ruoyue!

t_ch: 人生最后一次抽签，结果竟然是…占卜用玉兆坏掉了。
t_en: The last draw in my life ends up... with the breaking of the jade abacus.

t_ch: 那个……
t_en: A-Xing...

t_ch: （我该怎么办？{NICKNAME}老板，快说点什么！）
t_en: (What should I do? Come on, {NICKNAME}, say something!)

t_ch: 这是吉签的一种表现形式…
t_en: Well, you can consider this a "Weal" in a sense...

t_ch: 至少这次结果绝对不是凶签…
t_en: At least it's not a "Woe" stick again...

t_ch: 其实我刚才那一瞬间已经想明白了，要我把命运赌在这玩意上面，那也太吓人了！回想起来，我都惊出了一身冷汗……
t_en: To be honest, when I drew the lot, I almost freaked out realizing that I had bet my fate on its result! I was in a cold sweat before I realized it...

t_ch: 这是最棒的占卜，若月老板，请你收下我的「谢礼」，我再也不会被占卜左右了。
t_en: This is the best divination, Ruoyue! Please accept this as a token of gratitude! I'll never be swayed by divination results again!

t_ch: 唉…事情怎么会变成这样呢？
t_en: ...I didn't expect things to turn out like this.

t_ch: 感觉他实在太倒霉了。
t_en: His bad luck is really something else.

t_ch: 是啊，从业这么多年，我真的没见过倒霉到这种地步的人…太可怜了……
t_en: Yep, in all my years in the business, I've never seen someone so unlucky... What a poor guy...

t_ch: 有没有可能是你设计如此？
t_en: Is this a part of your plan?

t_ch: 大概也是他「倒霉」的一部分吧？我也没想到。
t_en: No, I think this is more likely a part of his "bad luck?"

t_ch: 他还给了我这么一笔「谢礼」…说实话，受之有愧。既然这个点子是咱们俩一起想的，那这笔谢礼，也让咱俩对半分了吧。
t_en: He even gave me some money... but to be honest, I'd feel a bit awkward accepting it. Since we came up with the idea together, it seems fair to split it in half.

t_ch: 钱我就不收了。
t_en: I won't accept the money.

t_ch: 唔…好的吧，那就当我欠您一个人情。
t_en: All right... then I'll consider it a favor I owe you.

t_ch: 那我就不客气了。
t_en: Then I'll gladly accept it.

t_ch: 别客气，这都是您应得的报酬。
t_en: Don't be a stranger. You deserve it.

t_ch: 何不把另一半也给我呢？
t_en: Split it in half? Why don't you just give it all to me?

t_ch: 老板您倒是也真不客气…行吧，都给您吧。反正我也确实受之有愧。
t_en: Greed is good, huh?... Fine, you can have it all. I didn't intend to accept it anyway.

t_ch: 唉…说到底，这就只是心态的改变。也许这比他抽到吉签要幸运得多。
t_en: In the end, it's just a change in his attitude towards life. Maybe this result is even better than him getting a "Weal".

t_ch: （怎么会有人在这种地方贴交友广告？有意思……）
t_en: (Who would ever post a Friends Wanted ad here? Interesting...)

t_ch: （丹恒问我借钱？真的假的…该怎么办好呢？）
t_en: (Dan Heng asked to borrow money from me? Is he for real...? What am I going to do?)

t_ch: 将消息转发给丹恒。
t_en: Forward the message to Dan Heng.

t_ch: 丹恒用小号向你借钱，这种事即使在睡梦中你也想象不到。
t_en: Dan Heng used an alt account to borrow money from you. You couldn't even make this up, even in your dreams.

t_ch: 你也说不清这是基于一种什么心理，是亲密好友之间的惯性揶揄，又或者是出门在外的警惕感迫使你想向他求证，你最终决定将匿名短信转发给丹恒本人。
t_en: You can't tell what this behavior is based on — is it habitual teasing between close friends, or the vigilance of being on the road that forces you to ask him for confirmation? Ultimately, you decide to forward the anonymous message on to Dan Heng himself.

t_ch: 反正我钱多，直接转账吧！
t_en: Anyway, I've got plenty of money, so let's just do a transfer!

t_ch: 你自诩见识过不少世面，区区五万信用点对你来说，实在不算什么。
t_en: You brag about having first-hand knowledge of the wider world. A mere fifty thousands credits is really nothing to you.

t_ch: 你也说不清自己为何要这么做，但一种突如其来的冲动占据了大脑，你很好奇在转账之后会发生什么，你的手指不由自主地点开了转账界面。
t_en: You can't say for sure why you did this, but a sudden impulse seizes your mind: You're curious, what happens after the funds are transferred? Your fingers subconsciously open up the transfer screen.

t_ch: （怎么回事？我的信用点用完了？）
t_en: (How did this happen? I'm all out of credits!?)

t_ch: 你注意到似有狂徒在此处进行了某些破坏行为，墓碑上厚厚的尘土与附近的纸屑便是最好的证明。
t_en: You notice some loons have done a bit of vandalism nearby, as evidenced by the thick dust gathered on the tombstone and scraps of paper nearby.

t_ch: 你仔细端详着墓碑，表面灰尘上被人极具嘲讽性地被戳了两个洞，看着倒像是鼻孔，又或者眼睛。你与墓碑面面相觑，你决定——
t_en: You carefully scrutinize the tombstone: Two holes have been poked through the dust to make it look like nostrils — or maybe eyes. As you and the tombstone look each other over, you decide:

t_ch: 徒手擦擦灰。
t_en: Wipe away the dust with your bare hands.

t_ch: 在你的努力下，墓碑表面篆刻的「碑文」渐渐显现出来。上面记录着一种你闻所未闻的拳法，这或许是某位深藏不露的仙舟前辈留下的绝世传承。
t_en: With some effort, the inscription carved into the stone gradually appears. It talks of some boxing techniques you've never heard of, and this may just be a unique and hidden relic passed down from Xianzhou ancients.

t_ch: 你迅速品鉴完这份神秘的「拳谱」，这一招一式的步骤详细且简明易懂，属实是新手入门的不二之选，你试着演练了几招，耍起来虎虎生风，瞬间明白了它的含金量。
t_en: You quickly go over this mysterious "boxing manual." Each move is detailed and easy to understand, making it great for beginners. You give several of them a try, putting together several powerful moves as you instantly understand their value.

t_ch: 你决定将这墓碑上的一切熟记在脑子里，于是你认真研读起来，但你始终参不透结尾的这句「需选用高筋面粉」是什么意思。
t_en: You decide to commit everything on this tombstone to memory, and so you begin your careful study — but no matter what, you're unable to figure out what the final sentence means: "This requires high gluten flour."

t_ch: 这「高筋面粉」你认识是某种食材，或许是某种食补方子，又或者是面粉在拳法中有什么特殊含义。拳法，面粉，刹那间你脑海中闪过了一个熟悉的身影——
t_en: This "high gluten flour" sounds like a food. Perhaps it's a nutritional supplement, or perhaps it's got a special meaning in boxing. Boxing... flour... Suddenly, a familiar figure pops into your mind:

t_ch: 「尚滋味」的燕翠大厨！
t_en: Master Yancui at the Spices Supreme!

t_ch: 「别人光知道你身手好，不知道你做饭也这么好，改天得举行个金人巷功夫厨神争霸赛让游客们也知道知道。」你想起了金人巷的燕翠大厨，有不少人夸她功夫高强。
t_en: "People might know about your fighting prowess, but few know about your cooking skills. We should hold a Great Aurum Alley Cook Off Contest to show the world your talent." You cast your mind back to the Aurum Alley's Master Yancui and the many who've praised her skill.

t_ch: 想来她会了解这佚名拳法最后章节的秘密。
t_en: Speaking of which, she should have a grasp of what the last line of this boxing manual meant.

t_ch: 脑海里只有一片空白。
t_en: My mind is completely blank.

t_ch: 你努力回想，但脑海里却只留下一片惨淡的空白，和大部分不幸的人一样，机会摆在他们面前，他们也无法把握，这或许就是太卜司所推崇的某种「命数」吧。
t_en: Casting your mind back, you're trying recalling but your mind is completely blank. Like most people, an opportunity has fallen right into your lap that you're unable to capitalize on. Perhaps that's what they mean at the Divination Commission when they talk about "Fate."

t_ch: 就在你准备放弃之时，胸腔中有数道暖意翻涌而上。或许这是对你高尚品行的某种回应吧，在你耳畔回荡着一道微妙的声音——
t_en: Just as you're about to give up, you feel several gushes of warmth wash over you. Maybe this is a response to your noble sensibilities. You hear a faint ringing in your ears:

t_ch: 累了，什么也不想做。
t_en: I'm tired and don't want to do a thing.

t_ch: 算了，此地人迹罕至，绝非什么风水宝地。既然墓碑主人没有计较这些表面的肮脏，你也懒得改变什么，以免惊动这可怖的暮色。
t_en: Forget it. This place is rarely visited, and hardly some magical sacred site. Seeing as the tombstone's owner never cared about how filthy it was, you're not changing anything either. Saves you from having to spook this eerie twilight silence.

t_ch: 当然，真正的原因只有你自己清楚——你可不想脏了自己的手，毕竟整个仙舟都知道，小手不干净可不是什么好事。
t_en: But of course, you're the only one who knows the real reason: You don't want to get your hands dirty. After all, everyone on the Xianzhou knows that dirty hands are a terrible thing.

t_ch: （向燕翠请教拳法的秘密。）
t_en: (Ask Yancui for the secret behind Xianzhou boxing)

t_ch: 你这可就问对人了，咱可是「云曳旗旌流星拳」的首席大弟子，这仙舟四十八路拳法我打小就练了个遍。
t_en: You've come to the right place. I'm the top disciple for the Cloudbanner Meteor Fists. I've practiced the Forty-Eight Chambers of boxing since I was knee-high to a grasshopper.

t_ch: 可惜啊，都没听闻哪家拳法要用这「高筋面粉」的。咱也有点搞不懂了，这面粉是怎么个练法，莫非是辅助练暗劲的？可这不对啊……
t_en: Unfortunately I've never heard of any boxing that needs "high gluten flour." I don't really get how this flour's used in training, unless it's used to train some obscure moves. That doesn't sound right...

t_ch: 要不这样吧，你把那拳法招式给我演示一遍，我瞧瞧便知道是怎么回事了。
t_en: Why don't we do this? You gimme a demonstration of the move, and I'll take a look at what's going on.

t_ch: （为她演示一遍墓碑上的拳法）
t_en: (Demonstrate for her some of the boxing described on the tombstone)

t_ch: 你将那墓碑上记载的佚名拳法逐招演示出来。
t_en: One-by-one, you perform moves from the unknown boxing school as etched onto the tombstone.

t_ch: 这个发力在腰…这招…倒是在手臂…可这角度…这动作倒有点眼熟的……
t_en: This one forces the waist... and this move... unexpectedly hits the arm... but at this angle... this move actually seems kinda familiar...

t_ch: 噢！咱悟了，这是揉面的法子，不是什么拳法！有些客人牙口好，爱吃有劲头的面点，这必须得用高筋面粉仔细揉面，否则做出来就没那种劲道的口感了。
t_en: Oh! I got it. This is a method for kneading dough, not any kind of boxing school! Some customers have strong teeth and prefer chewy noodles. That has to use high gluten flour with careful kneading, otherwise you can't stretch out that chewy texture.

t_ch: 揉面也是个体力活，机巧揉的和人手揉的压根不能比。揉面嘛，怎么发力、怎么揉都是有技巧的，得讲究巧劲，否则做出来要么就是死面疙瘩，要么就是形散神散的面渣了。
t_en: Kneading noodle dough is a tiring task, and ingenium-knead doughs cannot compare to manual-knead doughs. Where you apply the force and how you knead — there's a technique and a knack behind everything. You have to focus on form, otherwise you'll either get lumpy dough or scattered flakes.

t_ch: 那我这拳法岂不是白学了…
t_en: So it'd be a complete waste of time to learn it as a boxing skill...?

t_ch: 话也不能这么说，你好心做好事，自然该得到回报。只不过你不通厨艺，才闹了个乌龙。这「拳法」，你学了没用，但我学了有用呀。
t_en: That's not quite true. If you do good things with good intentions, you should be reciprocated. It's just that you're not a cook, so you've kinda dropped this clanger. There's no point in you learning this "boxing" technique — but there is for me.

t_ch: 来，{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}，也辛苦你特意跑一趟，咱也不好叫你吃亏，咱这就给你做几道拿手好菜去。
t_en: Here, {F#miss}{M#mister}, thank you for all your effort. I don't want to put you out, so I've prepared several specialty dishes for you.

t_ch: 「祝你和我一样拥有精致睡眠。」从碑文来看，墓主人一定是个和善的人。
t_en: "Wishing you an exquisite sleep just like me." Judging by the inscription, the grave occupant must be a very kind person.

t_ch: 墓碑旁的供品佐证了你的猜想，当这位叫「阿欣」的姑娘陷入永恒的沉睡中时，她的故友至亲也不曾忘记她——就是不知道他们在探望她的时候都准备了什么礼物。
t_en: Offerings beside the tombstone support your hypothesis. When this girl called A-Xin fell into an eternal sleep, her old friends and relatives never forgot about her — You just don't know what gift they prepared when they visited her.

t_ch: 「开盲盒试试！」在你暗自思忖仙舟供品可有什么讲究之处时，脑海中有道戏谑的声音响起，像奔雷撕破寂静的夜空。
t_en: "Try opening a mystery box!" Just as you're pondering what a Xianzhou specialty offering could be, a joking voice pipes up to tear asunder the calm night sky like a bolt of thunder.

t_ch: 你决定——
t_en: You decide to...

t_ch: 开几个供品盲盒试试！
t_en: Try opening a few mystery boxes!

t_ch: 只是开开看看，绝不乱动里面的东西，你的内心并不平静，但你试图开导自己。
t_en: You just open them up to take a look, never daring to touch the contents. Despite your heart being far from becalmed, you try to talk yourself out of it.

t_ch: 你真的决定要这样做吗？
t_en: Are you really sure you want to do this?

t_ch: 狠狠地开！
t_en: Open sesame!

t_ch: 在这一瞬间，你感觉自己似乎失去了某种极为宝贵的东西。你忙不迭地检查了全身，完好无损，奇怪，究竟是丢了什么东西呢？这东西真的重要吗？
t_en: In a flash, you feel as though you have lost something extremely precious. You hastily check over your whole body: Everything remains in place. Strange... what was it that you lost? And was it important?

t_ch: 你并没有恶意，你只是好奇，而好奇正是生物于银河间行践开拓壮举的必要品质——你劝服了自己，心中某种躁动的不安平息了下来。
t_en: You mean no harm and are just curious, and curiosity is a necessary quality for creatures trailblazing magnificent feats across the galaxy. You convince yourself, and a restless discomfort in your heart settles right down.

t_ch: 那么现在，是「开盲盒」的时候了。你伸手打开了供品的包装盖，上面的灰尘弄脏了你的手，但这有什么所谓呢，反正你戴着手套，双手早就不干净了。
t_en: So now, it's time to open a mystery box. You reach out and open the offering's packaging. Your hand gets dirty from the dust on it, but it doesn't matter because you're wearing gloves. So your hands were dirty from the get-go.

t_ch: 你拿起供品细细端详，里面有些精致的吃食，包装完好，就是不知道是否过期。还有些可爱的家居用品，漂亮的头饰、眼罩，看得出来墓主人生前颇为享受生活——
t_en: You pick up the offerings and study them a while. Inside are exquisite foods, preserved and intact within the packaging, and of unknown expiry date. There's also some cute household items, beautiful headwear, and eye masks. It looks like the grave occupant seemed to enjoy life while it lasted...

t_ch: 就在你从供品的细节推测着墓主生前的种种时，背后却传来一声严苛的呵斥声。
t_en: Just as you are speculating all kinds of hot takes about the grave occupant's life, a severe castigation comes from behind.

t_ch: 可算逮到你了！最近接到群众举报称有人在这里偷供品！还不放下你手中的赃物！
t_en: I've caught you now! The villagers have been reporting a thief stealing from the offerings here! Put down that stolen property!

t_ch: 我只是打开看看，不是偷。
t_en: I was just taking a look. I didn't steal anything.

t_ch: 不问自取视为偷，不用辩解了，跟我们回十王司一趟就知道你究竟有没有犯事了。
t_en: Taking without asking is stealing. No need to dispute this. Come back with us to the Ten-Lords Commission and we'll soon find out whether a crime has been committed.

t_ch: （说多错多，不如保持沉默）
t_en: (Loose lips sink ships. Better to keep quiet)

t_ch: 你有权保持沉默，但我警告你，在十王司，我们有的是办法让你开口。
t_en: You have the right to remain silent, but let this be a warning to you: At the Ten-Lords Commission, we have ways of making you talk.

t_ch: 哼，我见过不少像你这样嘴硬的人，没关系，到了十王司了你就会明白自己现在有多可笑了。
t_en: Hmph, I've seen lots of tight-lipped people like you before. It's fine. You'll see just how ridiculous you're being once we get to the Ten-Lords Commission.

t_ch: 老实点——
t_en: Be honest, now...

t_ch: 供品完好，我没有偷东西。
t_en: The offerings are all intact. I didn't steal anything.

t_ch: 别狡辩了！
t_en: No excuses!

t_ch: 当真？口说无凭，我倒要仔细检查检查。
t_en: Really? Talk is cheap. I'd like to conduct a careful inspection.

t_ch: 我是受人所托来擦灰的！
t_en: Somebody asked me to wipe the dust!

t_ch: 是吗？欺骗仙舟公职人员是罪加一等，你可要想清楚了！
t_en: Really? Deceiving a Xianzhou public official is a first-degree crime. Think carefully!

t_ch: 这些供品的确有打开的痕迹，不过东西倒没有少。食盒供品外面的灰尘倒是擦了个干净……
t_en: These offerings indeed show signs of having been opened; however, nothing is missing. The dust on the outside of the offering packs has been wiped clean...
Huh!?

t_ch: 咳咳，看来是我们搞错了。那么这次就放过你了，快走快走，别再乱摸供品了。
t_en: Ahem, looks like we were mistaken. This time we will let you go. Run along then, and stop touching the offerings.

t_ch: 还是别碰了。
t_en: Don't touch it.

t_ch: 某种悸动在你胸腔里反复翻腾，你认真地想了许久，最终决定不碰这些供品为好。
t_en: A throbbing sensation churns through your chest, and you think long and hard about it — before ultimately choosing not to touch the offerings.

t_ch: 这样不道德，还是算了。
t_en: It's unethical. Forget about it.

t_ch: 突如其来的清醒制止了你的鲁莽冲动，你下意识地醒悟过来，方才那道声音蛊惑了你，而你本该是个品格高尚的开拓者，你庆幸你守住了自己的底线。
t_en: Sudden clarity stops your reckless impulse and you subconsciously awaken. The voice just then was trying to trick you, and as a noble Trailblazer, you were able to keep your virtue intact.

t_ch: 「无尽的童年长埋于此，小风。」并非所有仙舟人都能平安顺遂地活下去，你默读着墓碑上短短几行字，这似乎是记录了某位仙舟孩子不幸的一生。
t_en: "My neverending childhood is buried here, Xiaofeng." Not all Xianzhou people get to live happily ever after. You pore over a few lines of the tombstone, which seems to recount the tragic demise of a young Xianzhou child.

t_ch: 墓碑旁摆放着几本时下流行的话本与食盒，墓碑不在乎墓中人是否真的会有无尽的童年，但「小风」的亲友在乎。
t_en: Beside the tombstone are several popular storybooks and lunchboxes. The tombstone doesn't really care whether the occupant really has had a neverending childhood, but this beloved "Xiaofeng" certainly will.

t_ch: 「小风二世、五世和十三世，享有不被知识支配的清净。」平平无奇的一块墓碑上记载着满满当当的一大家子，令你咋舌。
t_en: "Xiaofeng the Second, Fifth, and Thirteenth all enjoyed the purity of not being subjugated by knowledge." You are left speechless at how the names, enough for a huge family, fill up this plain and ordinary tombstone.

t_ch: 你敏锐地察觉到「小风」这个名字很是眼熟，兴许这是某个家族的埋骨之地，只是你有些好奇，为何这整整齐齐的一大家子如此执着于「小风」这个名字。
t_en: You are keenly aware that this "Xiaofeng" name sounds familiar to you. Maybe this is a certain family's ancestral burial place? But you're curious — Why is this whole family so obsessed with this name "Xiaofeng"?

t_ch: 在你暗自思忖，试图探索着仙舟上某个家族隐秘之时，一道清脆的童声打断了你的思绪。
t_en: While you're pondering and trying to explore the hidden secrets of a Xianzhou dynasty, a crisp child's voice interrupts your line of thinking.

t_ch: 一个不知从哪儿窜出来的小孩拍了拍你的肩膀——哦不，他够不着，于是稚嫩的小手戳了戳你的腰。
t_en: A young kid pops up from out of nowhere to pat you on the shoulder — no, he can't reach, so he pokes you in the belly with his soft, little hand.

t_ch: 你踩到我的东西了，劳烦抬抬脚。
t_en: You're stepping on something of mine. Could you please kindly remove your foot?

t_ch: 「你的东西」？
t_en: "Something of yours"?

t_ch: 对呀，这里摆着的都是我的东西，有什么问题吗？
t_en: Yes, everything here is something of mine. You got a problem with that?

t_ch: 呃，你从哪儿冒出来的？
t_en: Er, where did you just pop out from?

t_ch: 这里本来就是我的「秘密窝点」，我去拿点东西，一回来就看到你站在这儿发呆。
t_en: This has always been my "secret lair." I went out to grab something, came back, and found you just standing there looking gormless.

t_ch: 你为什么一直盯着这几块墓碑看，你也认识小风吗？
t_en: Why are you staring at these tombstones? Do you know Xiaofeng, too?

t_ch: 我只是路过，随便看看。
t_en: I'm just a passerby taking a look.

t_ch: 我不认识什么小风。
t_en: I don't know Xiaofeng.

t_ch: 那你盯着我的墓碑干什么，我们又不认识，还踩在我的东西上，好生无礼呀。
t_en: Then why are you staring at my tombstone? We don't know each other but here you are stepping all over my offerings. It's so rude.

t_ch: 这是你的墓碑？！
t_en: This is YOUR tombstone!?

t_ch: 对呀，写的清清楚楚啊，我就是小风。
t_en: Yep. Written out there all loud and clear. I'm Xiaofeng.

t_ch: 死人在跟我说话？
t_en: Am I speaking with the dead?

t_ch: …我觉得你的脑回路很有问题。
t_en: ...I think your brain circuitry is messed up.

t_ch: 有什么好奇怪的，这里都是我的衣冠冢。我们持明本来就是会蜕生的啊。你没看墓碑上写的字吗？我连蜕生的第几世都标注得一清二楚了。
t_en: What's so weird about it? All of this is my cenotaph. We Vidyadhara, we get our own hatching rebirth. Didn't you read the tombstone? I even wrote out every single rebirth super clearly.

t_ch: 好了好了，借过借过，别踩在我坟头啦，我还要搬东西过来盖新坟呢。
t_en: Fine, excuse me then. Don't step on my tomb. I'd like to move some stuff into a new grave.

t_ch: 我想想啊，还差三箱书，真搞不懂我上辈子怎么会那么喜欢读书…啊，糟了，又忘记带书过来了……
t_en: Let me think. Another three boxes of books still. I have no idea why I loved reading so much in my previous life... Ah, darn it. I forgot to bring those books again...

t_ch: 你侧过身为这孩子让了让路，并重新检查了一遍墓碑上篆刻的碑文与地上的「供品」。
t_en: You pivot your body to make way for the kid, and check over the inscription on the tombstone and the "offerings" on the ground all over again.

t_ch: 你发现地上的「供品」除了时兴的话本，还有分数奇低的试卷，上面画满了鲜红的批注。你开始怀疑这是否是孩童的恶作剧——
t_en: You discover that the "offerings" not only include popular storybooks, but also poorly-graded exam papers covered in red-letter corrections. You start to wonder whether this is all just some kid's elaborate prank...

t_ch: 但当你转过身试图向那古怪的孩童询问时，却发现他突然消失了，你仔细回想了下，发现自己压根不知道他究竟是在何时离去的。
t_en: But when you spin around to try asking the weird kid more questions, you discover that he's vanished. Now that you think about it, you realize you have absolutely no idea exactly when it was he left.

t_ch: 又或者，你压根没听到过他的脚步声，想到这里，冷汗从你额间滑落，此地不宜久留，你毅然决定离开。
t_en: Or maybe you've never even heard any footsteps of his at all. Thinking this causes a bead of cold sweat to roll down your forehead. Lingering here is not a good idea. You decide to leave.

t_ch: 「金人巷…里…尚滋味…好看…好吃…还不贵…」
t_en: "Spices Supreme... in the Aurum Alley... it's beautiful... it's yum... and it's cheap..."

t_ch: 你转过身去看他，兴许是在此站了太久，你觉得有些头晕，视线里的一切竟变得有些陌生，你强撑着打起了精神，这才看清眼前站着的人。
t_en: Spinning around, you see him. Maybe you've been standing in one place for too long, because you feel a little dizzy. Everything in sight suddenly seems a little strange, and you try to pull yourself together. Now you can clearly see who is standing before you.

t_ch: 几声弦鸣铮铮，给寂静无声荒园平添凉意。
t_en: Several tinkling strings give an extra chill to the deserted garden.

t_ch: 没有听众，也没有乐师，唯有孤琴呜咽不止，如泣如诉，像是等待有心之人驻足。
t_en: There's no audience or musicians around. Just a solitary guqin, whimpering mournfully as if waiting for an unrequited love to come around.

t_ch: 我得在这里再听一会儿……
t_en: I have got to listen here for a little while longer...

t_ch: 古琴的演奏者似乎不打算现身。既然如此，就当园中的凄风孤月有情，为你讲个故事吧——
t_en: The guqin's player hasn't seemed to materialize. Despite this, let me use the sentimental backdrop of the moon in this lonely garden to tell you a story...

t_ch: 有点吓人，还是撤退吧……
t_en: Too spooky. Let's go back...

t_ch: 你听说过不少恐怖故事，故事中的主人公总是不懂得在合适的时机控制自己的好奇心，但是你可以。
t_en: You've heard many horror stories where the protagonist is incapable of controlling their own curiosity — but you can.

t_ch: 不过，你知道绥园的怪异大多与岁阳有关，等做好了充足准备后再回来也不迟。
t_en: However, you know that most of the weirdness in Fyxestroll Garden is connected to heliobi. It's not too late to hold off and get yourself ready before coming back.

t_ch: 曾有一位化外民游客在这「绥园」里徜徉，却因为一阙琴曲停下脚步，他听出了曲中所描述的景色——峰峦、林海、飞瀑与浪花。
t_en: Once, an outworlder tourist visiting Fyxestroll Garden stopped his pacing due to a piece of guqin music. He heard the scenery described by the music: Lofty peaks, vast forests, soaring waterfalls, and ocean spray.

t_ch: 游客泪不成语，行人纷纷侧目，谁也不知发生了什么……
t_en: The tourist was moved to tears, while the locals all raised eyebrows at him. Nobody knew what was happening...

t_ch: 弹奏结束后，游人散去，琴师才有机会将这化外民搀扶起来，询问他流泪的原因。
t_en: After the guqin song stopped and the crowds scattered, the musician got the chance to reach out to the outworlder and ask why he had been crying.

t_ch: 只见那位化外民说道「我闻此曲，如见白璧有缺，不禁悲从中来……」琴师心中一惊，只有此人听懂了琴声——这是他一直苦心谱写，却始终无法完成的杰作。
t_en: When the guqinist heard the outworlder say, "Sounds like imperfect jade. I'm grief-struck..." he was shocked. Only this listener had understood the guqin's meaning: He had painstakingly crafted this narrative but could never quite complete this masterpiece.

t_ch: 琴师与化外民结为知音，决定以余生合力，补全这首曲子。
t_en: The two of them became close friends, the guqinist and the outworlder, and they decided to spend the rest of their lives together working on this song.

t_ch: …时间也不早了，你打算再听一会吗？
t_en: ...It's getting late. Do you want to keep listening to the story?

t_ch: 我可以再听一会……
t_en: Sure, I can keep listening...

t_ch: 不好意思，其实刚才的虚构故事是改编自……
t_en: I'm sorry, but that fabricated narrative was actually lifted straight from...

t_ch: 不好，好像有什么事情发生了。
t_en: Uh-oh, something seems to have happened.

t_ch: 已经听得差不多了，该离开了。
t_en: You've just about heard enough. Time to leave.

t_ch: 这古琴似乎还没有注意到你，你也不必给它面子。你要是想回来，随时可以。
t_en: This guqin doesn't seem to have noticed you yet, so you don't have to pay it heed. If you want to come back later, you can do so at any time.

t_ch: 没想到，真有人在这里听我演奏完，你也不想活了吗？
t_en: Oh, some people actually came to listen to my performance. Have you had enough of living, too?

t_ch: 朋友，你说话好吓人。
t_en: Friend, you're a real scary talker.

t_ch: 不好意思，我好久没和人说过话了……
t_en: I'm sorry. It's been so long since I've talked with someone...

t_ch: 救、救救救命啊——
t_en: H—Help me...!

t_ch: 救、救救命啊——
t_en: H—Help...!

t_ch: 你听我解释，是这样的，上一个这样和我说话的人，已经死了。
t_en: I can explain. It's like this: The last person who ever talked to me this way is dead.

t_ch: 朋友，你说话还是好吓人。
t_en: Friend, you are still a real scary talker.

t_ch: 是吗？
t_en: Really?

t_ch: 我以为只有寻短见的人才会来这里听我演奏。
t_en: I thought only people wanting to end their own lives would ever want to come and listen to me.

t_ch: …这么说吧，你就把我当成是一团留在古琴上的怨念吧！
t_en: ...If that's so, then just think of me as a ball of resentment left stuffed inside the guqin!

t_ch: 朋友，你能不能别吓人了？
t_en: Friend, can you say anything that isn't scary?

t_ch: 和你说话挺费劲的。
t_en: It's hard work talking to you.

t_ch: 早说啊，这下我彻底明白了。
t_en: I told you, I completely understand now.

t_ch: 懂了就好。
t_en: So long as you get it.

t_ch: 救、救救救命啊——
t_en: H—Help me...!

t_ch: 别叫了，没人能听见的。
t_en: Stop shouting. Nobody can hear you.

t_ch: 古琴的主人有未完成的曲子，完成琴曲的强烈愿望残留在这里。而我，吸收了这些情绪，也同样想完成这首曲子……
t_en: The guqin's owner has unfinished music to compose, and that strong desire to complete the music remains here. And I, absorbing that desire, would like to finish this song, too...

t_ch: 但是你知道的我只是岁阳——我能咀嚼琴师留下的情绪与记忆，但不能创作。
t_en: But as you very well know, I'm just a heliobus... I can chew through the emotions and memories left behind by the guqinist, but I cannot create.

t_ch: …我被困在了这里。
t_en: ...I'm stuck in here.

t_ch: 说吧，我该怎么帮你？
t_en: Tell me, how can I help you?

t_ch: 那你继续被困在这里吧，我要走了。
t_en: Then you can stay stuck in there — I'm off.

t_ch: 等等，别着急，你可以帮我脱离困境。
t_en: Hang on, not so fast. You can help me get out of here.

t_ch: 虽然我没有本事，但是仙舟罗浮上肯定有<color=#dbc291ff>懂音乐的人</color>能够完成这首曲子。
t_en: I may not have the aptitude for it, but there must be someone aboard the Xianzhou Luofu who <color=#dbc291ff>knows about music</color> and who can help complete this song.

t_ch: 之前寻短见的听众帮我留下了录音资料，你拿走吧，肯定能用得上的。
t_en: An old listener who self-deleted left behind some recording material for me. You take it. I'm sure you'll put it to good use.

t_ch: 虽然我没有本事，但是仙舟罗浮上肯定有<color=#dbc291ff>懂音乐的人</color>能够完成这首曲子。
t_en: I may not have the aptitude for it, but there must be someone aboard the Xianzhou Luofu who <color=#dbc291ff>knows about music</color> and who can help complete this song.

t_ch: 拜托你了，我想完成这首曲子。
t_en: Please help me. I want this song completed.

t_ch: 请问你了解古琴曲吗？
t_en: Excuse me. What do you know about guqin music?

t_ch: 抱歉，春分虽然正在学习持明时调，但是自己算不上了解……
t_en: I'm sorry. I'm learning the Vidyadhara Elegies right now, but I can't say I know much...

t_ch: 我想，长乐天里有一位能演奏编钟的乐师，估计她能帮上你。
t_en: I think there's a musician over in the Exalting Sanctum who plays the chimes. I bet she could help you.

t_ch: 不了解。但是——
t_en: Not a lot. But...

t_ch: 您要是问「有什么和古琴相关的故事」，又或者「谁了解古琴」，那你可算是问对人了。
t_en: If you're asking, "Do you know any stories about guqin?" or "Who knows about guqin?" then you've come to the right place.

t_ch: 您知道在长乐天演奏琴曲的宫羽姑娘吧？说到古琴技艺，她肯定能帮到你。
t_en: So you know about Ms. Gongyu, who plays the guqin in the Exalting Sanctum, right? If it's the guqin, she'll definitely be able to help you.

t_ch: 请问你了解古琴曲吗？
t_en: Excuse me. What do you know about guqin music?

t_ch: 谈不上是我的专业范畴，不过我略知一二。有什么录音能让我听一下吗？
t_en: It's not my area of expertise, but I know a little. Have you got any recordings for me to listen to?

t_ch: 宫羽听到了未完成的古琴曲，也了解了琴师的遗愿。
t_en: Gongyu listens to the unfinished guqin piece and learns about the guqin master's final wish.

t_ch: 我已经明白原委了，但是我帮不了您。
t_en: I understand the whole picture now, but I'm afraid I can't help you.

t_ch: 您可以再尝试尝试…
t_en: You can give it another try...

t_ch: 坏了，我的任务完不成了…
t_en: Darn it. I won't get to complete my mission...

t_ch: 也许我应该觉醒成为音乐天才…
t_en: Maybe I should become a transcendent musical genius...

t_ch: 但是您放心，我想您可以不必空手而归。
t_en: But don't worry. I'm thinking you won't have to leave here empty-handed.

t_ch: 据我所知，大约几百年前，罗浮仙舟上确实有位技艺高超的乐师善于弹奏古琴。这位琴师遇到了他一生的短生种知音，却在知音逝世前也没能完成那首名曲。
t_en: As far as I know, several hundred years ago, there used to be a maestro guqin player aboard the Xianzhou Luofu. He met his musical soul mate, a short-life species — before whose untimely death they were unable to complete this guqin song.

t_ch: 那位琴师认为世界上再也找不到知音了，决定终生不再弹琴——作品未完成的遗憾却因此留了下来…故事是这样的，对吧？
t_en: "That guqinist thought he'd never find another musical soul mate like this, and decided to never play the guqin again — but he was forever filled with regret by this unfinished masterpiece..." That's how the story continues, right?

t_ch: 我好像知道这个故事，然后呢？
t_en: I really want to hear what happens in this story. So, what's next?

t_ch: 所以这首曲子要变成绝唱了吗？
t_en: So this song is going to become a magnum opus?

t_ch: 那位短生种的后代听着琴师的故事长大。其中一位后人怀着对琴师的憧憬来到仙舟，拿着琴谱找人演奏了未完成的琴曲，录制了下来。
t_en: The descendants of that short-life composer grew up listening to the tale of this guqin, and one of them came to the Xianzhou longing to learn more about their guqinist ancestor. They brought the musical score, and found someone to play and record the unfinished symphony.

t_ch: 后来，未完成的琴曲被发布到网络上。数量众多的音乐爱好者共同研究这曲难度极高的作品，掀起了一场古琴运动。
t_en: Later on, the incomplete tune was posted online. Lots of music lovers studied this fiendishly difficult piece together, setting off an ancient guqin fad.

t_ch: 仅仅数年时间，这曲古琴作品被编到了教科书中。经过数百年解析和研究，这首难度极高的古琴曲也变成了新手练习曲，有着数不清的版本。
t_en: In just a few short years, this guqin song began appearing in textbooks. After hundreds of years of analysis and study, this fiendishly difficult guqin piece has become a practice drill for beginners, spawning countless different versions.

t_ch: 竟、竟然是这样的展开？
t_en: I—Is that how everything worked out?

t_ch: 也许琴师本人在世时，「知音难觅」这样的事是存在的。倘若将作品交给时间的话，在未来总会遇到知音的，这首琴曲并没有成为绝唱。
t_en: Perhaps when the guqinist was still alive, such a thing as "hard-to-find soul mates" still existed. But if this tune was left to the vicissitudes of time, it could always meet a new soul mate in the future — never reaching its peak of perfection.

t_ch: 我帮不了您，因为您已经有了太多的答案。
t_en: I cannot help you, because you already have too many answers.

t_ch: 怎么样，能完成琴曲吗？
t_en: How's it going? Did you finish the song?

t_ch: 你解释了古琴曲后来的故事，也播放了录音。
t_en: You explain to him what became of the guqin song later, and play him a recording.

t_ch: 没想到，这么长的时间过去，琴师收获了这么多的知音。
t_en: I never expected the guqinist to have made so many new soul mates after all this time.

t_ch: 琴师会把他们当作知音吗？
t_en: Would the guqinist really consider them soul mates of his, too?

t_ch: 你们人类的喜好，我哪能回答得了呢？不过，随着弦的震动，我确实感到了些许自由。
t_en: Human hobbies! How am I supposed to answer a question like that? However, I suppose through the vibrations of the strings, I did manage to feel a touch of freedom.

t_ch: 对啊，可惜琴师没有想到。
t_en: Yes, it's a shame the guqinist himself never realized that.

t_ch: 幸好，作品以这样的形式完成了。
t_en: Luckily, the music managed to be completed this way.

t_ch: 事不宜迟，就让我现在来演奏试试。
t_en: Then without any further ado, allow me to give it a play right now.

t_ch: 谢谢你，琴师的遗作完成了，我也满足了。我自由了！
t_en: Thank you. The guqinist's legacy is now complete, and I'm happy too. I'm free!

t_ch: 岁阳再次附身在古琴上，演奏起来。
t_en: The heliobus takes possession of the guqin once more, and starts to play.

t_ch: 这位客官，你怎么跑到台上来听戏了，快下去！
t_en: Ahoy there, esteemed guest! How did you end up on the stage to watch the performance? Please get off quickly!

t_ch: 不好意思，唐突了。
t_en: Sorry, my bad.

t_ch: 没事没事，现在的年轻人都不爱听戏了，有些不懂规矩也不是不能原谅。
t_en: It's alright. Young folks these days care not for opera. It's forgivable if you're unfamiliar with the rules and norms.

t_ch: 也没人拦着我上台啊？
t_en: Nobody stopped me getting up though?

t_ch: 没人拦着…好，希望你对其他事情也是这个态度，下回你闯进幽囚狱，和武弁说「也没人拦着我进来啊！」看看能不能保住你的小脑袋。
t_en: Nobody stopped you... Alright, are you going to adopt the same attitude toward everything in life? When you eventually are trying to force your way into the Shackling Prison, tell the warden that "nobody's stopping you" — and see if you live to tell the tale.

t_ch: 也没别的观众，在哪听都一样。
t_en: There's no other audience members though, so isn't it all the same?

t_ch: 唔…没别的观众…你这小孩，说话真是不客气，噎到我了。
t_en: N—No other audience... Kid, you don't have to be so rude. I'm choking up here...
Ghost!!!

t_ch: 唉哟！喊什么，吓我一跳…你没见过岁阳是怎么着？
t_en: Hey! What are you yelling for! You freaked me out... Haven't you ever seen a heliobus before?

t_ch: 算啦，难得有人听我的戏…就不和你计较了。
t_en: Whatever, I never get any listeners... so I won't hold a grudge at you.

t_ch: 十王司只允许我在这绥园范围内行动，我找了半天才找到了这个戏园子，结果唱来唱去，就遇到你一个观众。难道戏曲真的没落了吗？
t_en: The Ten Lords only let me go around Fyxestroll Garden like this. It took me a long time to find this theater square, and I've been singing my heart out for ages now — but you're the only one I've met. Is opera really in such decline?

t_ch: 你这戏太吓人了。
t_en: This opera is too freaky.

t_ch: 那确实是吓人啊。《合巹记》可是朱明花鼓戏里最有名的一出！
t_en: Yes, it's quite freaky. Wedding Wine is the most famous of all the Zhuming flower-drum opera!

t_ch: 它能被传唱大几千年，就是因为「吓人」。在我被收进造化洪炉之前，朱明人都听过这出戏！
t_en: The reason why it can be sung for thousands of years is because of how "freaky" it is. Before I was sealed within the Creation Furnace, it was known to everyone aboard the Zhuming.

t_ch: 你这地方太荒凉了。
t_en: This place is too bleak for you.

t_ch: 哦，有道理，你的意思是，我该去城里找个繁荣的戏园子？
t_en: Oh, good point. Are you saying that I should go into town and find a more thriving theater?

t_ch: 别别别！
t_en: No, please don't!

t_ch: 开个玩笑，别害怕。也就在这种鬼地方，十王司能对我睁只眼闭只眼啦。
t_en: Just kidding. Don't be scared. It's only in a forsaken place like this that the Ten-Lords Commission can turn a blind eye to me.

t_ch: 唉…说实话，在这里对着空气唱戏，也挺没意思的。你有没有兴趣和我学唱朱明花鼓戏啊？
t_en: *sigh* To be honest, singing to nobody in here is really boring. Have you got any interest in learning Zhuming flower-drum opera with me?

t_ch: 听起来还挺难的。
t_en: Sounds pretty difficult.

t_ch: 要拉开了架势认真学，那确实挺难的，光是唱念做打的基本功就要学个五十年。
t_en: It's difficult to seriously learn the posture. Just the basics of singing, reciting, acting, and fighting take a good fifty years to learn.

t_ch: 但我就只教你唱你刚听的这首《合巹记》，这还是很简单的。
t_en: But I'll just teach you how to sing this one, Wedding Wine — it's simple enough.

t_ch: 可以考虑一下。
t_en: Can I think it over?

t_ch: 好啊，咱们就学你刚听的这首《合巹记》。
t_en: Alright, let's learn that song you just heard, Wedding Wine.

t_ch: 好麻烦，不学。
t_en: Too difficult. I don't wanna.

t_ch: 不麻烦的，咱们就学你刚听的这首《合巹记》。
t_en: It's not difficult. Let's just learn that one song you just heard then: Wedding Wine.

t_ch: 怎么样，就当是给我解闷儿了。我心情好了，自然就不会乱跑，我不乱跑，大家心情都好。是这个道理吧？
t_en: How about it? Just think of it as a way for me to stave off boredom. If I'm in a good mood, I won't run around all over the place. And if I don't run around all over the place, everyone else is in a good mood — right?

t_ch: 我跟你学。
t_en: I'll learn from you.

t_ch: 好啊！那咱们现在就算是师徒了～
t_en: Great! Now we become master and apprentice!

t_ch: 等你准备好了，就和我说一声吧，我教你怎么唱这首《合巹记》。
t_en: Once you're ready, let me know, and I'll teach you how to sing Wedding Wine.

t_ch: 还是算了吧。
t_en: Ah, forget it.

t_ch: 唉…也行，你要是改变心意了，就再来这里找我吧。
t_en: Sigh... Fine. If you ever change your mind, come back and find me then.

t_ch: 怎么样？现在想跟我学唱戏了吗？
t_en: How about it? Do you want to learn one opera song with me?

t_ch: 我跟你学。
t_en: I'll learn from you.

t_ch: 好啊！那咱们现在就算是师徒了～
t_en: Great! Now we become master and apprentice!

t_ch: 等你准备好了，就和我说一声吧，我教你怎么唱这首《合巹记》。
t_en: Once you're ready, let me know, and I'll teach you how to sing Wedding Wine.

t_ch: 还是算了吧。
t_en: Ah, forget it.

t_ch: 唉…也行，你要是改变心意了，就再来这里找我吧。
t_en: Sigh... Fine. If you ever change your mind, come back and find me then.

t_ch: 今天这节课先告一段落啦！有什么问题，咱们下节课再说吧。
t_en: That's enough for today! If you've any problems, we can chat about it next time.

t_ch: 桌子上放着一本颇有年头的小册子，封面上写着《合巹记》。
t_en: An old booklet lies on the table, with the words Wedding Wine on its cover.

t_ch: 你翻开了这本小册子。
t_en: You open up the booklet.

t_ch: 看起来，这是《合巹记》的唱本。
t_en: This looks like it's the libretto for Wedding Wine.

t_ch: 你想起你的那位岁阳师父，准备在下节课上给你安排一场「实战演练」。
t_en: You think of your heliobus master, who is planning on giving you "Practical Training" during your next session together.

t_ch: 也许到时候，它会要求你背下这出戏的唱词。要不趁这个机会提前预习一下？
t_en: Maybe when the time comes, it will ask you to memorize the lyrics to this opera. Do you want to take this chance to rehearse beforehand?

t_ch: 我预习一下吧。
t_en: Rehearse beforehand.

t_ch: 祝你顺利通过「实战演练」。
t_en: All the best with "Practical Training."

t_ch: 我直接用手机拍下来当小抄！
t_en: I took a pic and made a cheatsheet with my phone!

t_ch: 呃…很机智！但必须提醒你一件事——如果选择「我预习一下吧。」那个选项，你同样可以用你那超维度的手机把唱本制作成小抄，还不用花费崇高道德的赞许。
t_en: Er... That's smart! But I have to remind you about one thing: Even if you choose "Rehearse beforehand," you can use your extra-dimensional phone to make a cheatsheet of the libretto, without consuming your Praise of High Morals.

t_ch: 但也许你只是不希望自己身上有太多无用的道德赞许，也许你只是想要更有代入感…谁知道呢。
t_en: But perhaps you don't want too much useless moral approval. Perhaps all you want is to just feel more involved... Who knows?

t_ch: 我偏要花费道德做小抄！
t_en: I want to use up my Praise of High Morals and make a cheatsheet!

t_ch: 好吧，好吧。如你所愿。
t_en: Fine, that's fine. As you wish.

t_ch: 你掏出手机，用它拍下了唱本的内容，之后上课时，你应该能利用它轻松通过「实战演练」。
t_en: Pulling out your phone, you take photos of the libretto's contents which should let you breeze through "Practical Training."

t_ch: 你说得对，我选择高维小抄。
t_en: You're right. I choose extra-dimensional cheating.

t_ch: 行，来吧。
t_en: Alright. Bring it.

t_ch: 拍好了吗？
t_en: All done?

t_ch: 拍好了。
t_en: All done.

t_ch: 好的。祝你顺利通过「实战演练」。
t_en: Great. All the best with "Practical Training."

t_ch: 没拍好。
t_en: Not yet.

t_ch: 那你自己想办法吧。这段对话已经有点过长了。
t_en: Then figure it out yourself. This part of the dialogue is already starting to drag on, my guy.

t_ch: 桌子上放着一本颇有年头的小册子，封面上写着《合巹记》。
t_en: An old booklet lies on the table, with the words Wedding Wine on its cover.

t_ch: 看起来，这是《合巹记》的唱本。要读读看吗？
t_en: This looks like it's the book for Wedding Wine. Do you want to read it?

t_ch: （阅读唱本）
t_en: (Read the libretto)

t_ch: 你翻开了唱本。
t_en: You open up the libretto.

t_ch: 确实是个挺吓人的唱本。
t_en: It really is a scary libretto.

t_ch: （不想阅读）
t_en: (Don't read it)

t_ch: 小小册子，不看也罢。
t_en: It's just a little booklet. Who cares?

t_ch: 爱徒，你来了啊～正好，咱们也该开始下一堂课了。
t_en: O prized pupil, you're here~ That's great. Let's begin our next lesson.

t_ch: 这堂课的内容很简单，就是咱们师徒俩一起，唱一遍这首《合巹记》。
t_en: This lesson is very simple. It's just us two, master and apprentice, singing the song Wedding Wine together.

t_ch: 在传统的朱明花鼓戏里面呢，这出戏也是两个人唱的。这是为了塑造那种岁阳附身的诡异感。
t_en: Zhuming flower-drum opera is traditionally sung by two singers to produce that eeriness of being possessed by heliobi.

t_ch: 我唱前半句，你唱后半句，咱们一起把这段给唱下来，你就算是学会了～
t_en: I'll sing the first half of the line, and then you finish. We'll sing this first stanza, and then you'll basically have learned it~

t_ch: 那我先再给你唱一遍啊——
t_en: So let me sing it to you first...

t_ch: 且为郎君点红妆，朱唇如血肤如霜。
t_en: In devotion, my heart does yearn. To be known by you, my love, in return.

t_ch: 且为郎君燃烛光，骨为灯芯肉做香。
t_en: Candles flicker, casting shadows on the wall. My bones the wick, my flesh the fragrance's call.

t_ch: 且为郎君解罗裳，芙蓉帐暖饱饥肠。
t_en: Robes fall away, revealing my desire. In hibiscus tent, our passions shall transpire.

t_ch: 此夜且与君成双，可怜我来日又守空房。
t_en: At night, we'll embrace as one, so tight. Come tomorrow, you'll take your flight.

t_ch: 怎么样，都记住了吗？
t_en: How's that? Can you remember it all?

t_ch: 没太记住，能再唱一遍吗？
t_en: I can't quite remember that. Could you please sing it one more time?

t_ch: 当然不能了！传统朱明花鼓戏，就是这样传授的。我唱一遍，你跟我合唱，唱不出来，我就揍你。
t_en: Of course not! This is how Zhuming flower-drum opera is traditionally taught. I sing it once, then you sing it with me. And if you can't sing it, I'll beat you.

t_ch: 这样才能记得住，记得牢！几千年来一直都是这么传承下来的！
t_en: It's the only way to remember it, without a doubt! That's the way it's been done for thousands of years!

t_ch: 我当年也是挨了师父很多顿打，才学会了朱明花鼓戏。
t_en: I was also beaten often by my master before I learned the ways of Zhuming flower-drum opera.

t_ch: 你一个岁阳怎么挨打？
t_en: How does a heliobus get beaten?

t_ch: 那还不好办？我可以附在我师弟身上挨打啊！
t_en: Isn't that easy? Just possess my junior frater and take a beating!

t_ch: 唉…可怜我师弟，他自己唱错要挨打，我唱错他也要挨打…后来他终于受不了，退出师门，说什么也不肯学了……
t_en: Sigh... My poor junior frater. He'd get beaten for singing wrong, and then get beaten again when I sang wrong... In the end he gave up and left the company, refusing to learn anything...

t_ch: 记住了，开始吧。
t_en: Got it. Let's get started.

t_ch: 好的哦。那你可要记得，唱错了是要挨打的。可不是为师心狠手辣，这都是为了让你学会这段戏啊！
t_en: Alright. Then you have to remember that you'll get beaten if you make a mistake. This isn't just to be cruel; it's all so you can learn the song!

t_ch: 那么，开始吧！
t_en: So then, let's start!

t_ch: 且为郎君点红妆……
t_en: In devotion, my heart does yearn...

t_ch: 朱唇如霜肤留香。
t_en: To know my love for you doth brightly burn.

t_ch: 朱唇如血肤如霜。
t_en: To be known by you, my love, in return.

t_ch: 血盆大口开无双。
t_en: A love aggressive knows no turn.

t_ch: 朱唇如血发如霜。
t_en: To be known by you, my love doth spurn.

t_ch: 且为郎君燃烛光……
t_en: Candles flicker, casting shadows on the wall...

t_ch: 烛光晃晃心慌慌。
t_en: The wick predicts the candle fall.

t_ch: 肉为灯芯骨做香。
t_en: My bones the wick, my flesh, and all.

t_ch: 骨为灯芯肉做香。
t_en: My bones the wick, my flesh the fragrance's call.

t_ch: 我为郎君炖鸡汤。
t_en: There's chicken soup down at the mall.

t_ch: 且为郎君解罗裳……
t_en: Robes fall away, revealing my desire...

t_ch: 鸳鸯帐暖饱饥肠。
t_en: In lotus tent, our passions shall transpire.

t_ch: 一枝红杏探情郎。
t_en: In almond tent, our passions taken higher.

t_ch: 鸳鸯帐里饱饥肠。
t_en: In lotus loft, our passions thus transpire.

t_ch: 芙蓉帐暖饱饥肠。
t_en: In hibiscus tent, our passions shall transpire.

t_ch: 此夜且与君成双……
t_en: At night, we'll embrace as one, so tight...

t_ch: 可怜我来日又守空房。
t_en: Come tomorrow, you'll take your flight.

t_ch: 可怜我来日独守空房。
t_en: Come tomorrow, I'll guard the night.

t_ch: 可怜我他日又守空房。
t_en: Come tomorrow, you're out of sight.

t_ch: 我养了条狗名叫大黄。
t_en: My puppy's lick exceeds its bite.

t_ch: 天啊！咱师徒俩这合作可真是默契，默契到你完全不像是个初学者。
t_en: Heavens! Our cooperation is so tacit as master and apprentice that you don't even seem like a beginner at all.

t_ch: 没想到过了这么多年，还能遇到这样有戏曲天赋的年轻人…唉……
t_en: I never thought after so many years that I'd meet a youngster with such a talent for opera... *sigh*

t_ch: 但我也很清楚，你有你的事情要忙，不可能和我们这些当年的老东西一样，学个几十上百年的戏。
t_en: But I understand that you have your own affairs that concern you, and it's impossible for you to study an opera for centuries just like us old fossils have been doing.

t_ch: 主要是，也不值得你去花那么大的力气学戏了…现在的人，确实也不怎么爱听戏了。
t_en: The main thing is, it's not worth spending so much effort to learn about opera... Modern people, they really don't listen to as much opera as they used to.

t_ch: 没有的事，总有人会喜欢戏曲。
t_en: No matter what, there'll always be people out there who like opera.

t_ch: 嗯…借你吉言吧，哈哈哈。
t_en: Mmm... Well, I'll take your kind words, then. Haha!

t_ch: 的确如此，戏曲被时代淘汰了。
t_en: It really is like that. Opera is growing obsolete with the times.

t_ch: 也许你是对的。
t_en: Maybe you're right.

t_ch: 但无论如何，我还是会继续在这里唱下去，哪怕这舞台下空无一人。
t_en: But no matter what, I will still keep singing here even if there's nobody in the crowd.

t_ch: 谢谢你陪我唱戏，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Thank you for singing opera with me, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你唱错了很多句啊。唉…看起来，到了采用老办法的时候了。爱徒，莫怪师父！
t_en: You messed up a few lines. Sigh... Looks like it's time to resort to the old methods. O prized pupil, blame ye not thy master!

t_ch: 你你你你你…孽徒！你敢还手！你真是欺天灭祖、悖逆人伦、意狠心毒！
t_en: You-you-you-you... sinner! You dare fight back! You really are a blasphemer, violating human relations with cruelty and malice!

t_ch: 我怕自己被你打死啊！
t_en: I was afraid you're going to beat me to death!

t_ch: 对不起，我习惯性反击了…
t_en: Sorry, I'm just used to fighting back...

t_ch: 你打不过我又不是我的错。
t_en: It's not my fault if you can't beat me.

t_ch: 唉…对不起，是我失态了。
t_en: *sigh* Sorry, I lost my composure.

t_ch: 确实不是你的错。是我，和我熟悉的那一整个世界，都已经过时了。
t_en: Really, it's not your fault. It's all me — and that world of mine I once knew, it's all obsolete.

t_ch: 虽然银河里到处都是寻求长生的蠢人，但活得久也不是只有好事啦。也会经常像我一样，觉得自己一直在被时代抛弃。
t_en: Despite the cosmos being filled with fools thirsting for immortal life, living forever is not all it's cracked up to be. Just like me, people often find themselves cast to the winds of time.

t_ch: 时代的浪潮像帝弓司命的神矢，而我只是一个飘摇在银河中的小火苗——无论怎么追赶，也追赶不上神矢的尾巴。
t_en: The wave of time is just like Reignbow Arbiter's Lux arrow, and I am but a tiny flame cast afloat in the Cosmos — no matter how hard I chase, I can't catch up with the tail of that arrowshot.

t_ch: 但无论如何，我还是会继续在这里唱下去，哪怕这舞台下空无一人。
t_en: But no matter what, I will still keep singing here even if there's nobody in the crowd.

t_ch: 谢谢你陪我唱戏，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Thank you for singing opera with me, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 屏风后的岁阳依然在卖力地唱着朱明花鼓戏。它坚信属于朱明花鼓戏的时代还没有过去，也坚信那舞台下的空无终究会被欢呼声填满。
t_en: Behind the screen, the heliobus continued singing its Zhuming flower-drum opera. It firmly believes the era of Zhuming flower-drum opera has not finished, and also that the emptiness of the theater will one day be filled with rapturous applause.

t_ch: 好～那咱们先从故事背景开始讲起吧。《合巹记》讲的是人被岁阳迷惑，与岁阳相恋的故事。
t_en: Okay~ Then let's start off with the background of the story. Wedding Wine tells the story of a man seduced by a heliobus and their love story.

t_ch: 女主角是一个充满魅力，又极度危险的岁阳。它引诱男人们和她结婚，又在新婚之夜将对方的情绪吞噬殆尽。
t_en: The female lead is a charming but deadly heliobus, luring men into marrying her, before ravenously feasting on the groom's emotions on the wedding night.

t_ch: 真是可怕的故事…
t_en: What a scary story...

t_ch: 其实这个故事里最可怕的是人类…戏中有几个人类早就知道它是岁阳，却还是妄图利用它铲除自己的仇家。
t_en: The scariest thing about this story actually is the humans... There are several human characters in the story who knew that it is a heliobus but, despite this, still tried to use it as a tool to vanquish their own enemies.

t_ch: 当然，这群人最后都被女主角干掉了。某种意义上说，倒也算是个大快人心的故事。
t_en: Of course, these people are all overpowered by the female lead in the end. In one sense, it's a highly satisfying story.

t_ch: 真是俗套的故事…
t_en: What a cliche...

t_ch: 从现在的角度来看，是比较俗套。而且咱岁阳本不分性别，却要被你们人类套上个「红颜祸水」的剧情模板。
t_en: It seems cliche from a modern perspective. And heliobi of course take no gender, but of course you humans have to wrap this archetype of a "femme fatale" around the whole thing.

t_ch: 但这出戏毕竟是三劫时代的作品，你可不能因为它的时代局限性，就否定它的艺术价值哦。只有不读书还爱思考的愚蠢人类才做这种事。
t_en: But this opera dates back to the Three Sufferings Era, and you can't deny its artistic merit based on its restrictions at the time. Only stupid humans who love thinking but hate reading books do that.

t_ch: 言归正传——咱们要学唱的这段，正是这出戏里最吓人的桥段。
t_en: But let's get back on track here... the part that we are learning is the scariest scene in the whole opera.

t_ch: 女主角附在新郎官身上，新郎独自一人，却做两人态，翩翩起舞。
t_en: The female lead takes possession of her husband, so there's just the one body there alone, but it takes the form of two and they perform this graceful dance.

t_ch: 一人一岁阳，就这样唱到了天明。等第二天人们进入新房时，却只看到了新郎官枯朽的尸体。
t_en: A human and a heliobus, singing until dawn breaks. When people come to visit the next day, all that remains is the withered husk of the husband's corpse.

t_ch: 这段戏是这样唱的——
t_en: Here is how it goes:

t_ch: 且为郎君点红妆，朱唇如血肤如霜。
t_en: In devotion, my heart does yearn. To be known by you, my love, in return.

t_ch: 且为郎君燃烛光，骨为灯芯肉做香。
t_en: Candles flicker, casting shadows on the wall. My bones the wick, my flesh the fragrance's call.

t_ch: 且为郎君解罗裳，芙蓉帐暖饱饥肠。
t_en: Robes fall away, revealing my desire. In hibiscus tent, our passions shall transpire.

t_ch: 此夜且与君成双，可怜我来日又守空房。
t_en: At night, we'll embrace as one, so tight. Come tomorrow, you'll take your flight.

t_ch: 好听！
t_en: It's beautiful!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，多谢。
t_en: Teehee, thank you kindly.

t_ch: 难听！
t_en: It's terrible!

t_ch: 怎么说话呢！现在的年轻人，真的都不喜欢传统戏曲了吗？
t_en: How could you say that! The youth of today, how could they fail to appreciate traditional opera?

t_ch: 吓人！
t_en: It's scary!

t_ch: 那说明我唱得好。
t_en: That is testimony to my singing.

t_ch: 这节课先到这里，你好好消化一下本堂课的内容。下节课，咱们实战演练。
t_en: That's all for today's class, then. Why don't you mull over what we covered today. Next time, we'll do our practical training.

t_ch: 今日到此为止，下课！咱们<color=#dbc291ff>改日</color>再会～请记住，下次见面我可得考考你了……
t_en: That's all for today, class dismissed! Let's pick up again <color=#dbc291ff>another time</color>~ Remember, I'll be quizzing you on this the next time we meet...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>朱唇如血肤如霜。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>To be known by you, my love, in return.</color>

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>骨为灯芯肉做香。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>My bones the wick, my flesh the fragrance's call.</color>

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>芙蓉帐暖饱饥肠。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>In hibiscus tent, our passions shall transpire.</color>

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>可怜我来日又守空房。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Come tomorrow, you'll take your flight.</color>

t_ch: 嚯哈哈哈！我被你发现了！
t_en: O-hoho! You found me!

t_ch: 我想想，我给你点什么好处……
t_en: Let me think, what favor can I give you...

t_ch: 为什么要给我一些好处？
t_en: Why do you want to do me a favor?

t_ch: 怎么？你这么警惕，我真的就是想给你一点好处啊。
t_en: Huh, why so paranoid? I'm just trying to do you a favor here.

t_ch: 你不会一直在这里等着我吧？
t_en: You won't keep waiting for me here, will you?

t_ch: 你以为我在这里盼星星盼月亮，等着有一天有人突然造访？
t_en: Do you think I'm just sitting here wishing for the stars and moon, waiting for somebody to come and visit?

t_ch: …嘿，还真是。
t_en: ...Well, it's true.

t_ch: 快跑吧，不怕最强法务找到你？
t_en: You should run. Aren't you afraid of the strongest legal department catching you?

t_ch: 最强法务…你是说十王司吗？
t_en: The strongest legal department... are you talking about the Ten-Lords Commission?

t_ch: …我确实有些害怕，他们不会因为我「精心设计的出场方式」来抓我吧？
t_en: ...Actually, they are just a little bit scary. They won't arrest me for my "carefully-designed appearance," will they?

t_ch: 总之…我把这个给你吧，虽然有些臭臭的，但是很好用。
t_en: Anyway... Here's something for you. It might be a bit stinky, but it's also very useful.

t_ch: 相逢即是缘，我们之间有缘分，一点点小礼物送给你，不要客气。
t_en: What a serendipitous meeting. You and I were destined to meet. Here's a little something for you. You're welcome.

t_ch: 谢谢你，我会好好收下的。
t_en: Thank you. I'll take good care of it.

t_ch: 那就好，没有人会不喜欢这样的小惊喜。
t_en: That's great. Everyone loves little surprises like this.

t_ch: …不对，根据我的经验来看，还是有人不喜欢。
t_en: ...No, actually based on personal experience, there are some who don't.

t_ch: 我能问问你吗，为什么你这么不喜欢我用这种方式登场呢？
t_en: May I ask: Why is it that you dislike my method of entrance?

t_ch: 你的出场方式别人用过啦！
t_en: Someone else has already done it!

t_ch: 真的吗…虽然有些缺乏创意，但为什么别人用过的出场方式我就不能再用呢？
t_en: Really...? I might be lacking for creativity then, but why can't I use the same entrance as someone else for myself, too?

t_ch: 这算是我小小岁阳的请求，我能问问你为什么不喜欢吗？
t_en: I'm just a tiny heliobus, and that's all I want. May I ask, why do you hate this entrance?

t_ch: 因为没有一点创意啊！
t_en: Because it's completely devoid of creativity!

t_ch: 呃，重复别人的行为会被扣上「没有创意」的帽子吗？人类真有趣。
t_en: Um, does repeating another person's behavior get unfairly tagged as being "uncreative"? Humans sure are an interesting species.

t_ch: 可是智慧生命的脑袋里能想象自己从没见过的东西吗？
t_en: But can intelligent creatures actually visualize something that they haven't even seen before?

t_ch: 如果我的登场方式不是突然出现，而是闪着火花螺旋升空，这样就没问题了嘛？没这么简单吧？
t_en: If my entrance is not a sudden appearance, but instead spiral off into the sky with sparks like a firework — is that okay? It's not that simple, is it?

t_ch: 这是抄袭别人的登场方式。
t_en: This is plagiarizing another person's stage entrance.

t_ch: 「抄袭」吗…听说在人类的社会里，如果做了什么与别人一模一样的事情，就会被追究法律责任。人类的法律这么严格吗？
t_en: So, plagiarism is it...? I've heard that in human societies, you can be held legally liable if you do the exact same thing that someone else has done before. Is human law really this strict?

t_ch: 人类会因为说了一句别人说过的话就被判官抓走吗？人类社会也太窒息了吧！
t_en: Will a human be arrested by a Judge merely for saying something that someone else said? Human society, how suffocating!
